evals.expected_output: A review identifying MVVM violations (business logic in code-behind, direct UI manipulation, service injection into Page, DispatcherTimer in View) with a refactored DashboardViewModel.cs extracting all logic into proper ViewModel with BindableBase.
assertions: Must identify business logic in code-behind as a CRITICAL MVVM violation
Must flag the 5 service dependencies injected into the Page constructor
Must flag DispatcherTimer management in code-behind — should use PeriodicTimer in ViewModel
Must flag direct UI element manipulation (PublishedCount.Text, EventRateLabel.Text, etc.)
Must flag FindResource() called on every tick — should be cached
Must flag string interpolation in log call ($"Failed to start: {ex.Message}")
Must flag bare catch(Exception) — should use project exception hierarchy
Must produce refactored code using BindableBase with SetProperty pattern
Must mention that hardcoded 2-second interval violates ADR-021 (use IOptions<UiSettings>)
Refactored ViewModel must use RelayCommand for Start/Stop instead of click handlers

evals.expected_output: A review identifying performance anti-patterns: unbounded channel, LINQ allocation in hot path, string interpolation in logging, missing CancellationToken, blocking Wait() in DisposeAsync, missing flush timeout, and missing error handling.
assertions: Must flag unbounded channel — project standard is BoundedChannel with DropOldest (ADR-013)
Must flag .ToList() allocation inside the flush loop (hot path allocation)
Must flag string interpolation in LogDebug — use structured logging with semantic parameters
Must flag missing CancellationToken in ReadAllAsync and WriteAsync calls
Must flag _flushLoop.Wait() as blocking — should be await _flushLoop
Must flag missing flush of remaining batch items when channel completes (data loss)
Must flag missing error handling in the Task.Run lambda (fire-and-forget without catch)
Must flag LogDebug in production hot path — use counters instead
Must flag missing batch size configurability (hardcoded 100)

evals.expected_output: A comprehensive review covering provider compliance, error handling, performance, and cross-cutting concerns with specific findings for reconnection logic, rate limiting, JSON source generators, config reload, and more.
assertions: Must flag missing reconnection logic — no retry on transient WebSocket failures
Must flag IOptions<T> instead of IOptionsMonitor<T> for settings that may change at runtime
Must flag JsonSerializer.Serialize without source-generated context (ADR-014 violation)
Must flag JsonSerializer.Deserialize without source-generated context (ADR-014 violation)
Must flag string interpolation in LogInformation — use structured logging
Must flag CancellationToken.None passed to ReceiveAsync — should forward the ct parameter
Must flag missing sequence validation on incoming events
Must flag new byte[4096] allocation — buffer should be reused or pooled
Must flag _ws! null-forgiving operator — should guard against null with proper state check
Must flag missing integrity event emission on WebSocket disconnect
Must flag DisposeAsync not cancelling outstanding operations before closing
Must flag no symbol validation in SubscribeAsync

evals.expected_output: A review identifying error handling violations (bare Exception, missing CancellationToken, swallowed OperationCanceledException, IOptions vs IOptionsMonitor, string interpolation in logs, missing date validation, missing idempotency), with refactored code.
assertions: Must flag throw new Exception — should use project exception hierarchy (e.g. BackfillException or ProviderException)
Must flag catch(Exception) is too broad — catches OperationCanceledException which should propagate
Must flag missing CancellationToken parameter — BackfillAsync should accept and forward ct
Must flag IOptions<BackfillSettings> — should be IOptionsMonitor for hot config reload
Must flag string interpolation in both LogInformation and LogWarning calls
Must flag missing date range validation (from < to, no future dates)
Must flag missing idempotency check — re-running backfill may create duplicate data
Must flag missing FlushAsync call on _sink after writing all events
Must flag that providers should be ordered by ProviderPriority config, not arbitrary IEnumerable order
Must flag missing progress reporting for long-running backfills

evals.expected_output: A review identifying test quality issues: async void tests, vague naming, shared mutable state, Task.Delay for timing, meaningless assertions, missing disposal, missing CancellationToken, and suggesting proper patterns.
assertions: Must flag async void test methods (TestPipeline, TestShutdown) — xUnit won't await these, tests silently pass
Must flag static shared _sharedChannel — shared mutable state between tests violates isolation
Must flag Task.Delay(500) — use TaskCompletionSource or SemaphoreSlim for deterministic synchronization
Must flag Assert.True(true) in TestShutdown — meaningless assertion, test passes even if shutdown fails
Must flag test name 'ShouldWork' — should follow MethodName_Scenario_ExpectedResult convention
Must flag test name 'TestPipeline' — too vague, rename to describe specific scenario
Must flag missing CancellationToken with timeout in async tests — tests can hang indefinitely
Must flag pipeline.Dispose() instead of await pipeline.DisposeAsync() — IAsyncDisposable not properly awaited
Must flag missing using/await using for disposable pipeline — resource leak on test failure
Must flag no test for backpressure behavior (channel full scenario)

evals.expected_output: A review identifying F# interop issues, non-exhaustive pattern matching, JSON source generator violation, thread safety problems, and logging anti-patterns.
assertions: Must flag non-exhaustive F# discriminated union matching — missing Reconnecting and RateLimited cases
Must flag the _ => 'Other' catch-all as dangerous — new DU cases will silently fall through
Must flag JsonSerializer.Serialize without source-generated context (ADR-014 violation)
Must flag string interpolation in LogInformation — use structured logging
Must flag Dictionary<string, ProviderStatus> _cache is not thread-safe — multiple providers may update concurrently
Must flag UpdateStatus only checks IsConnected — should handle all status transitions (Reconnecting, RateLimited)
Must flag missing null/option handling — F# types may return FSharpOption at the boundary
Severity: non-exhaustive matching and thread safety should be CRITICAL or WARNING

evals.expected_output: A review identifying: throwing bare Exception kills the ingest loop (should emit integrity events), _lastSequenceNumber not thread-safe, string interpolation in all log calls, LINQ hot-path allocations, missing sort-order enforcement, missing timestamp validation, and validation failures should be integrity events not just log messages.
assertions: Must flag throw new Exception as CRITICAL — killing the ingest loop on sequence gap is wrong; should emit an integrity event through the pipeline instead
Must flag bare Exception type — should use MarketDataException or IntegrityException from Core/Exceptions
Must flag _lastSequenceNumber as not thread-safe — needs Interlocked.Read/Exchange or locking if called from multiple threads
Must flag string interpolation in all LogWarning and LogError calls — use structured logging with semantic parameters
Must flag .Any(), .Max(), .Min() LINQ calls as hot-path allocations — this runs per order book update
Must flag missing bid descending / ask ascending sort-order enforcement
Must flag that negative quantity and crossed book should emit integrity events through the pipeline, not just log
Must flag missing timestamp validation — should detect future timestamps or unreasonably stale data
Must flag that Validate returns void with no way to signal issues to the caller — should return a validation result or emit events

evals.expected_output: A review identifying multiple cross-project dependency violations: WPF APIs (System.Windows.Media) in Ui.Services, UWP WinRT APIs (Windows.Security.Credentials) in shared service, direct reference to Wpf.ViewModels project, direct reference to Uwp.Helpers project. Also: IOptions instead of IOptionsMonitor, string interpolation in logging, reflection-based JSON serialization, and bare Exception catch.
assertions: Must flag using System.Windows / System.Windows.Media as CRITICAL — Ui.Services must be platform-neutral, no WPF APIs
Must flag using Windows.Security.Credentials as CRITICAL — UWP/WinRT APIs in shared service violates platform neutrality AND UWP is legacy
Must flag using MarketDataCollector.Wpf.ViewModels as CRITICAL — reverse dependency from Ui.Services to Wpf host
Must flag using MarketDataCollector.Uwp.Helpers as CRITICAL — cross-host dependency and UWP is deprecated
Must flag SolidColorBrush usage as platform-specific — belongs in WPF View layer only
Must flag PasswordVault as UWP-only API — should use platform-neutral credential abstraction
Must flag IOptions<SecuritySettings> — should be IOptionsMonitor for hot config reload
Must flag string interpolation in LogInformation and LogWarning — use structured logging
Must flag JsonSerializer.Serialize without source-generated context — ADR-014 violation
Must flag bare catch (Exception) — should catch specific exceptions and use project exception types
